purpose: Researcher-ready local input assembled from baseline signal files, optional LLM exploration reports, and the research source catalog. This is research material, not a product concept.

baseline.description: Fixed recurring collectors for comparable daily coverage.

editorShortlist.description: Heuristic shortlist for Research Editor / Concept Strategist. The LLM may override it but must explain why.

sources.name: Devpost hackathon projects
sources.notes: Primary source for hackathon-style product seeds. Preserve award context, project URL, demo/repo links, input-output transformation, and anti-clone boundary.

sources.name: AI hackathon winner pages
sources.notes: Use only public result pages and primary hackathon/showcase pages. Do not treat sponsor platforms as source products.

sources.name: community, university, and company demo showcases
sources.notes: Good for non-obvious small ideas. Record uncertainty if award status is unclear.

sources.name: GitHub Trending
sources.notes: Prefer recent momentum and demoable behavior over total star count. Avoid already-canonical mega-products.

sources.name: GitHub Search
sources.notes: Use public metadata only. Preserve query, stars, forks, issues, pushed_at, topics, and why the repo is small/rising.

sources.name: Hacker News Show HN / Launch HN
sources.notes: Use story metadata and linked repo/product pages by default. Do not ingest long comment bodies unless explicitly approved.

sources.name: Product Hunt launches
sources.notes: Capture metadata, positioning, and audience reaction. Do not use already dominant products as source cards.

sources.name: Hugging Face Spaces
sources.notes: Strong source for small interactive AI demos. Preserve task, input, output, likes/usage signals when visible.

sources.name: Indie maker launches and product galleries
sources.notes: Use as qualitative market signal, not proof of market size.

sources.name: AI tool directories and public product galleries
sources.notes: Directory entries are weak unless they link to a live demo, product page, or clear public reaction.

sources.name: Google Trends / current events
sources.notes: Use only as remix target context. Do not let broad trends replace small product source cards.

sources.name: public civic and education context
sources.notes: Useful for target themes after a small product value core has been selected.

expectedSources: community, university, and company demo showcases
Hacker News Show HN / Launch HN
Indie maker launches and product galleries
AI tool directories and public product galleries

sourceProductCards.name: antivibe
sourceProductCards.concept: Learn what AI writes, not just accept it. A Claude Code skill that turns AI-generated code into educational deep dives.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: Learn what AI writes, not just accept it. A Claude Code skill that turns AI-generated code into educational deep dives.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Users need a focused way to turn github oss project inputs into a concrete output or decision without starting from a blank chat.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: developers and agent builders
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Project-specific input, prompt, dataset, workflow context, or configuration from the github oss project setting.
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: A visible demo artifact, workflow result, dashboard, generated output, or decision-support view.
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: demoable product-source artifact
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: The project packages ai, claude-code, claude-code-skill, code-explanation signals into a narrow input-to-output workflow, making the mechanism inspectable enough for Hackbase.ai to remix without copying the original product.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: User provides a narrow task or context, then reviews a concrete result, dashboard, preview, briefing, or generated artifact.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It is product-shaped rather than a broad platform: the value can be studied as a transferable mechanism.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: GitHub stars: 653 GitHub forks: 42 Created: 2026-04-10T18:07:20Z Pushed: 2026-06-06T12:59:50Z Query: ai education learning created:>2025-07-08 pushed:>2026-05-24 stars:>30 This gives enough public evidence to revisit the project and decide whether it belongs in the permanent source index.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: Query: ai education learning created:>2025-07-08 pushed:>2026-05-24 stars:>30
attentionProof: Query: ai education learning created:>2025-07-08 pushed:>2026-05-24 stars:>30
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Generated by the product-source refresh preparer as a reviewable candidate with public source evidence.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: This is a candidate from public project/demo surfaces and should still be reviewed before promotion if it resembles a major established product.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Transfer the pattern of turning github oss project context into a concrete artifact, dashboard, or decision aid while changing the domain, audience, and branding.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: GitHub OSS project to concrete artifact
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: The useful seed is the narrow transformation pattern and visible proof, not the original product category.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: Copying antivibe's name, positioning, source domain, visual identity, or implementation too directly.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy antivibe's brand, exact workflow, domain claims, or implementation; use only the abstract value mechanism after review.
bestRemixTargets: learning artifact
local decision helper
creator workflow dashboard
public explainer

sourceProductCards.name: Lolaby
sourceProductCards.concept: Turns a child's own drawing into a personalized sung lullaby. A parent or teacher uploads the artwork, and the app writes custom lyrics about what the child drew and sings them back with instrumental accompaniment.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: Child's drawing plus their name in, a sung personalized lullaby with lyrics and a 'what Lola saw' explanation out.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Removes the creative labor of inventing fresh, personal bedtime content for a specific child; generic lullabies are not about the child's own imagination.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Parents, kindergarten teachers, and caregivers managing bedtime or nap routines for young children.
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: A child's drawing (canvas sketch or photo upload), the child's name, and an optional 'what do they love?' text field.
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Generated lullaby lyrics plus a sung audio track with instrumental accompaniment, and a short description of how the drawing was interpreted.
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Playable audio file (sung lullaby) with visible lyrics and an interpretation note.
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Chains three compact local models: MiniCPM-V 4.6 (1.3B) vision model interprets the drawing, a fine-tuned Llama 3.2 3B writes lyrics via llama.cpp, and Kokoro 82M TTS synthesizes the singing voice layered over custom DSP-generated instruments (guitar, piano, harp). Runs CPU-only with no cloud APIs.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Upload/draw -> name the child -> generate -> listen together at bedtime -> regenerate with different loves/instruments.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It grounds generative output in a child's own artifact (their drawing), so the AI output feels like a mirror of the child's imagination rather than generic content; the 'Lola saw...' explanation builds trust in the interpretation step.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: 22 likes and 10 community discussions on the Space during the Build Small Hackathon 2026; earned multiple hackathon badges (Off the Grid, Well-Tuned, Llama Champion) for its fully local CPU-only pipeline.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: 22 likes on the Hugging Face Space (observed 2026-07-08)
10 community discussion threads on the Space
Build Small Hackathon 2026 badges listed in README: Off the Grid, Well-Tuned, Off-Brand, Llama Champion, Sharing is Caring, Field Notes
attentionProof: 22 likes on the Hugging Face Space (observed 2026-07-08)
10 community discussion threads on the Space
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Clean example of child-artifact-in, family-ritual-artifact-out: personalization anchored in something the user made, with an explanation step that makes the AI interpretation inspectable.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Solo/small-team hackathon entry hosted as a free Gradio Space; no company, no funding, built on sub-4B local models.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Interpret a user-made artifact (drawing/photo) with a vision model, then re-express it in a completely different comforting medium (song), with a visible 'what the AI saw' trust step.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: A child's drawing becomes the seed of a song about that child.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Personalization sourced from the child's own creative output rather than from a profile or preferences form, sung rather than shown.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy 'AI lullaby generator for kids' with a name field and skip the drawing-grounded interpretation that makes it personal.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the lullaby-for-kids framing; transfer the pattern of turning a user-made artifact into a comforting artifact in another medium (e.g. grandparent's recipe into a story, garden photo into a seasonal poem-song).
bestRemixTargets: Turn a pet's photo and quirks into a personalized ballad for the owner
Turn a child's LEGO build photo into an epic narrated saga
Turn a patient's day summary into a calming evening reflection audio for elder care

sourceProductCards.name: CODA
sourceProductCards.concept: An AI that finishes the song you quit on: upload a 15-30 second voice memo of an unfinished song and get back a finished-sounding track that continues in the same key, tempo, and style.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: Unfinished 15-30s song clip in, seamlessly extended finished-sounding stereo track out.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Musicians accumulate graveyards of abandoned voice-memo song sketches; finishing them requires arrangement skill and time they do not have.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Amateur and semi-pro musicians with folders of unfinished phone recordings, seeking instrumental continuation without vocal synthesis.
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: A 15-30 second audio clip (voice memo, phone recording, or bounce) of an incomplete song.
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: A stereo 44.1 kHz track where the original clip is spliced seamlessly into an AI-generated continuation.
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Downloadable finished audio track with level-matched crossfade at the seam.
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: librosa DSP extracts key, tempo, and meter directly from the audio; Stable Audio 3 Small Music (0.6B) performs waveform-level inpainting to generate the continuation; custom stitching applies loudness matching and equal-power crossfading; a quality scorer evaluates crest factor, dynamics, and RMS across multiple candidate generations and rejects artifacts like synth bursts or silence collapse.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Record/upload clip -> auto analysis -> generate several candidate continuations -> best candidate auto-selected -> listen and download.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: Instead of generating music from text, it treats the user's abandoned fragment as the ground truth and only fills the gap — plus it ships an explicit automated taste gate (quality scorer) that discards bad generations before the user hears them.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: 11 likes on the Space during the Build Small Hackathon 2026; README badges (track:wood, offgrid, fieldnotes) show it placed in the creative track with a fully documented small-model pipeline.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: 11 likes on the Hugging Face Space (observed 2026-07-08)
Listed on the HF music-generation category page alongside major spaces (observed 2026-07-08)
Build Small Hackathon 2026 achievement tags in README: offgrid, track:wood, offbrand, fieldnotes
attentionProof: 11 likes on the Hugging Face Space (observed 2026-07-08)
Listed on the HF music-generation category browse page (observed 2026-07-08)
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Textbook 'complete the user's abandoned work' mechanism with a self-critique loop; both moves transfer far beyond music.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Hackathon-scale Gradio Space by a small maker using a 0.6B model; single-purpose by design, no company behind it.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Take the user's abandoned fragment as fixed ground truth, inpaint only the missing remainder in matching style, and gate outputs with an automated quality scorer before showing them.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Finish what the user started instead of generating from scratch.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Waveform-level continuation anchored to DSP-extracted key/tempo plus a reject-bad-candidates scorer, rather than text-to-music generation.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy 'AI finishes your song' and bolt a text-to-music model on, losing the anchoring analysis and the quality gate.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the song-finishing positioning; transfer fragment-anchored completion plus candidate scoring to another abandoned-work domain.
remixableThemes: seamless splicing of human and AI output
bestRemixTargets: Finish the knitting/craft pattern you abandoned from a photo of the half-done piece
Complete a half-written family recipe from the steps already written, matching the cook's style
Continue an abandoned sketchbook drawing in the artist's own line style

sourceProductCards.name: PawMap
sourceProductCards.concept: A collaborative city map of stray animals: community members photograph strays, and AI identifies each animal and checks whether it has been sighted before, building persistent per-animal profiles over time.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: Phone photo of a stray animal with GPS in, a re-identified animal profile pinned on a shared urgency-coded city map out.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Independent rescue volunteers lose track of which stray is which across sightings, so help efforts are duplicated or dropped; there is no shared memory of a city's stray population.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Independent animal rescue volunteers and community members in cities (built around Brasília, Brazil).
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Mobile photos of stray dogs or cats with GPS coordinates.
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Persistent animal profiles (species, breed, color, condition, distinctive marks) with sighting history, help history, and condition tracking on an interactive map.
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Interactive Leaflet.js map with color-coded urgency pins and per-animal profile pages.
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Llama-3.2-11B-Vision analyzes the photo; Nemotron Nano structures findings into JSON (species, breed, color, condition, marks); all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings (384-dim, computed locally) enable cosine-similarity matching at a 0.80 threshold to re-identify previously sighted animals; SQLite stores profiles rendered on a Leaflet.js map.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: See a stray -> snap photo -> AI matches or creates a profile -> pin appears on shared map -> volunteers coordinate help and log condition updates on repeat sightings.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: Embedding-based re-identification gives street animals persistent identity across many strangers' photos — turning disconnected acts of noticing into a longitudinal civic dataset.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: 11 likes on the Space in the Build Small Hackathon 2026 Backyard AI track; NVIDIA-sponsor achievement tags and published trace dataset ('Sharing is Caring') gave it visibility in hackathon coverage.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: 11 likes on the Hugging Face Space (observed 2026-07-08)
Build Small Hackathon 2026 Backyard AI track entry with NVIDIA sponsor and Off-Brand/Sharing-is-Caring achievements per README
attentionProof: 11 likes on the Hugging Face Space (observed 2026-07-08)
Build Small Hackathon 2026 Backyard AI track entry with sponsor/achievement tags per README
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Demonstrates crowd-photo re-identification as a mechanism for giving ephemeral real-world entities persistent identity — a strong, rarely used pattern for civic and nature apps.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Hackathon-built community tool for one city, SQLite-backed, run by a small maker; no funding or company.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Crowd-submitted photos + vision extraction + embedding similarity re-identification turn scattered sightings of moving real-world subjects into persistent tracked profiles on a shared map.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Give strays a persistent identity from strangers' photos.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Re-identification via local embeddings (is this the same dog?) rather than mere detection (this is a dog), enabling longitudinal care history.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy 'report a stray on a map' as a form-filling CRUD app and skip the re-identification memory that makes profiles persistent.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the stray-animal mapping app; transfer photo-based re-identification of recurring real-world subjects to another domain (trees, potholes, graffiti, market stalls).
bestRemixTargets: Neighborhood street-tree health tracker where residents' photos build per-tree care histories
Community bicycle/asset spotting map re-identifying stolen bikes from sighting photos
Migratory bird feeder log that re-identifies returning individual birds season over season
missingFields: note: live demo showed a runtime error at observation time

sourceProductCards.name: Dream Customs (Dream QA)
sourceProductCards.concept: A dream journal that converts last night's dream into a 'Morning Ticket' — a small, concrete, optional 5-minute action for today, grounded in specific details ('anchors') extracted from the dream.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: Dream told via text, sketch, image, or voice in; a Morning Ticket with one grounded 5-minute Today Tip out.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Dream journaling usually dead-ends in vague interpretation; this converts a fuzzy emotional residue into one tiny actionable step, explicitly avoiding therapy claims.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: People who remember their dreams and want playful morning reflection, not therapy or diagnosis.
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: A dream recollection as text, image/sketch, voice, or a mix; plus an answer to one AI follow-up question.
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: A Morning Ticket: a Today Tip (small optional action), a reflection grounded in the dream's own details, and the follow-up exchange.
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: A structured 'Morning Ticket' card per session.
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Extracts up to three 'dream anchors' from multimodal input, asks a single contextual follow-up question, then generates grounded guidance. MiniCPM-V-4.6 handles image/sketch analysis and MiniCPM5-1B handles text, summaries, and tip generation; bilingual EN/ZH interface.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Wake -> record dream in any modality -> answer one follow-up question -> receive Morning Ticket -> optionally do the 5-minute action.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: The anchor-extraction + single-question + tiny-action format is a disciplined scaffold that keeps generative reflection concrete and bounded — a deliberate anti-horoscope design in a domain notorious for vagueness.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Build Small Hackathon 2026 dual-track entry (Thousand Token Wood + Backyard AI) with 56 discussion threads on the Space, indicating unusually heavy judge/community interaction.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: 3 likes on the Hugging Face Space (observed 2026-07-08)
Listed in Build Small Hackathon 2026 org space index
attentionProof: 3 likes on the Hugging Face Space (observed 2026-07-08)
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Shows how to make AI reflection on subjective inner-life material concrete and safe: extract anchors, ask one question, output one tiny optional action.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Hackathon Gradio Space on 1B-class models by a small maker; explicitly positioned as playful, not a funded wellness platform.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Anchor extraction from fuzzy personal input + exactly one clarifying question + one small bounded action output keeps generative advice grounded and non-prescriptive.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Turn last night's dream into one 5-minute action today.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: A ticket-shaped output contract (anchors, one question, one tip) instead of open-ended interpretation.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy 'AI dream interpreter' chat with mystical readings, losing the anchor/one-question/one-action discipline.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the dream-interpretation framing; transfer the anchors + one question + one tiny action contract to other fuzzy inputs (moods, meeting vents, journal rants).
bestRemixTargets: Post-argument cooldown app: vent in, one repair action out
Garden mood ticket: photo of your garden in, one 5-minute seasonal task out
Commute reflection: voice note about your workday in, one boundary-setting micro-action out

sourceProductCards.name: Borderless
sourceProductCards.concept: An agentic relocation researcher: describe your citizenship, education, budget, and goals, and it researches 3-4 candidate destination countries in parallel, returning visa pathways, costs, timelines, and risks on an interactive 3D globe with cited official sources.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: Personal profile (citizenship, education, budget, goals) in; a cited multi-country relocation report on an interactive 3D globe out.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Immigration research means days of cross-referencing scattered official sites per country; this parallelizes and consolidates it into one comparable, cited report.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Professionals seeking skilled work visas, students exploring study abroad, anyone evaluating relocation feasibility across countries.
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Profile via guided intake form or chat: citizenship, education, work history, budget, timeline, goals.
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: A structured report of recommended countries with visa pathways, required documents, timelines, costs, risks, and citations to official sources.
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Interactive 3D globe (MapLibre) plus a structured cited report per country.
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: LangGraph workflow on Qwen3.6-27B via HF Inference: a Planner selects 3-4 destinations from the profile, parallel Researcher agents investigate each using web search (Exa), scraping/crawling (Firecrawl), and country metadata lookups, and a Consolidator merges findings into one comparable report.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Fill intake or chat -> watch parallel research progress -> explore results on the globe -> drill into per-country pathways and citations.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: Planner/parallel-researchers/consolidator is applied to a deeply personal, high-stakes consumer decision — and the geographic 3D globe output makes multi-option research spatially comparable instead of a wall of text.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: 5 likes on the Space in the Build Small Hackathon 2026 Backyard AI track; a rare consumer-life-decision agent among mostly dev-oriented agent entries.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: 5 likes on the Hugging Face Space (observed 2026-07-08)
Listed in Build Small Hackathon 2026 org space index (Backyard AI track per README)
attentionProof: 5 likes on the Hugging Face Space (observed 2026-07-08)
Build Small Hackathon 2026 Backyard AI track entry per README tags
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Best-in-corpus example of parallel agentic research applied to a personal life decision with spatial output — transfers to any 'compare N places/options with citations' problem.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Hackathon-scale Gradio Space; no company, uses free-tier inference; single maker per hackathon rules.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Profile intake -> planner picks a shortlist -> parallel researcher agents with search/scrape tools -> consolidator merges into a cited, comparable report rendered spatially.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Parallel research agents turn a life question into a cited country comparison.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Consumer immigration as the agent use case, with a 3D globe as the report surface instead of chat text.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy 'AI immigration advisor' as a chatbot answering from model memory without live research or citations.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the immigration/visa positioning; transfer planner->parallel-researchers->consolidator with cited spatial output to another multi-option life decision (schools, retirement towns, sports clubs).
bestRemixTargets: University/scholarship pathway researcher for international students on a world map
Retirement town comparison agent with cost/healthcare/climate citations on a region map
Van-life route planner researching seasonal work + campsites per region in parallel

sourceProductCards.name: OpenMythos
sourceProductCards.concept: A local-first security auditor: paste codebase content and a fine-tuned 27B model returns a multi-level vulnerability report, visual dependency risk paths, a threat-level assessment, and verifiable hotfix patches — with zero cloud API dependencies.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: Pasted codebase content in; vulnerability report, dependency risk visualization, threat level, and hotfix patches out.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Small teams cannot afford proprietary security platforms and do not want to ship their source to cloud scanners; this runs the audit locally.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Software engineering teams and solo developers wanting pre-exploitation detection and remediation without sending code to third parties.
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Pasted source code / codebase content.
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Vulnerability report with severity levels, dependency risk path visualization, threat declaration, and automated patch suggestions.
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Multi-level vulnerability report plus verifiable hotfix patch diffs.
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Qwen 3.6-27B fine-tuned via SFT on cybersecurity corpora (GitHub Advisory, VulnHub, BigVul-Filtered, security papers) with 32K+ token context; runs fully locally, and pairs detection with generated patches rather than findings alone.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Paste code -> receive tiered findings and risk graph -> review generated patch -> apply and re-check.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: Domain-specific SFT on a mid-size open model plus local-only execution reframes security audit as a private commodity, and the emphasis on emitting verifiable patches (not just findings) closes the loop.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: 155 likes and 8 community discussions — the top-liked entry in the Build Small Hackathon 2026 index; surfaced in hackathon coverage as a flagship Backyard AI example.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: 155 likes on the Hugging Face Space, top of the hackathon org index (observed 2026-07-08)
Described in Build Small hackathon search coverage as a featured vulnerability-hunting project
attentionProof: 155 likes on the Hugging Face Space (observed 2026-07-08)
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: The corpus's single devtool slot: shows fine-tune-plus-local-privacy as a differentiation mechanism and report-plus-fix as an output contract.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Open-source Apache 2.0 hackathon entry positioned explicitly as an alternative to proprietary platforms; small maker, paused free-tier Space.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Fine-tune a mid-size open model on domain incident corpora, run locally for privacy, and always pair each detected problem with a generated, verifiable fix.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Private local expert that finds problems and hands you the patch.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Detection and remediation fused into one local artifact pipeline, trained on public incident data.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy 'AI code security scanner' as a GPT wrapper that lists issues without patches, local execution, or domain training.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the code-security scanner; transfer the detect+fix+local-privacy triad to another sensitive-document domain (contracts, medical intake forms, tax filings).

Home-network privacy checkup that finds misconfigurations and emits the exact settings changes

sourceProductCards.name: Color Grade Transfer
sourceProductCards.concept: Instant cinematic tone matching: give it your photo plus any reference image whose 'look' you love, and it applies the reference's color grade to your image without shifting content or pixels.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: Source image + reference image in; source image re-graded with the reference's color palette out.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Manual color grading requires skill and time in Lightroom/DaVinci; matching a specific reference look is especially fiddly for non-professionals.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Content creators, photographers, and filmmakers who want a specific cinematic look without manual grading.
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Two images: a source to edit and a reference whose color grade should be copied.
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: The source image with the reference's palette and tone applied, content unchanged.
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Downloadable re-graded image.
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Qwen-Image-Edit-2511 (20B) with two LoRA adapters: a custom color-grade-transfer LoRA fine-tuned on synthetically generated cross-mapped pairs (same palette applied to different images, mapped bidirectionally to avoid content bleeding), plus a lightning LoRA for 4-step inference; VAE dimension alignment prevents pixel shifts. Runs locally on GPU.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Upload source -> upload reference look -> generate in seconds -> compare and download; iterate with different references.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: The synthetic training trick — applying identical palettes to different images and cross-mapping them — manufactures a paired dataset for a task with no natural ground truth, isolating style from content.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: 11 likes in the Build Small Hackathon 2026 with dual-track placement and multiple achievement badges; featured on the hackathon org page as an example submission.

sourceProductCards.name: Sema — Dyslexia-Friendly Reading & Writing Assistant
sourceProductCards.concept: Three everyday tools for people with dyslexia: ReadEasy simplifies any text and reads it aloud, VoiceOut turns spoken words into a written message in the right tone, and SnapGuide answers questions about a photographed screen or form with step-by-step highlighted guidance. Supports English and Kiswahili.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: Text, speech, or a photo of a confusing form in; simplified audio-backed text, tone-appropriate written messages, or highlighted step-by-step guidance out.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Bills, forms, and digital interfaces are hostile to people with reading difficulties; existing accessibility tools rarely cover the write-back and navigate-a-form directions.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: People with dyslexia or reading difficulties handling everyday text, bills, forms, and screens, including Kiswahili speakers.
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Text to simplify, spoken words to transcribe, or a photo of a screen/form plus a voice/text question.
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Simplified/summarized text with audio playback; a written message in appropriate tone; step-by-step visual guidance with highlighted elements.
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Simplified text + gTTS audio, drafted messages, and annotated guidance steps.
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Gradio app routing three task types to small open models: Qwen2.5-7B for simplification and tone-appropriate drafting, MiniCPM-V for image/OCR screen understanding, Whisper-large-v3 for speech recognition, gTTS for read-aloud; no data stored.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Pick the tool matching the friction (read / write / navigate) -> provide text, voice, or photo -> get an accessible version -> act on it immediately.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It covers the full literacy loop — comprehension in, expression out, and real-world navigation — rather than the usual read-aloud-only assistive tool, and it treats a low-resource language (Kiswahili) as first-class.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Build Small Hackathon 2026 Backyard AI entry with OpenBMB 'Best MiniCPM Build' and 'Best Demo' tags in its README; a rare assistive-tech entry in the cohort.

sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Bundle the three directions of a struggle (understand incoming, produce outgoing, navigate interfaces) into one assistant scoped to a specific underserved audience and language.

sourceProductCards.name: PitchFight AI
sourceProductCards.concept: A pitch practice arena: deliver your startup pitch by text or voice, pick a judge persona (Skeptical VC, Technical Judge, Hackathon Judge) and difficulty, then survive multi-round adversarial follow-up questions and get a scorecard of strengths and vulnerabilities.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: Spoken or typed startup pitch in; adversarial judge Q&A rounds plus a structured scorecard of strengths, vulnerabilities, and fixes out.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Founders rarely get realistic hostile-question practice before demo day; friends are too nice and real investors are a one-shot audience.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Student builders and early-stage founders preparing for hackathons, demo days, and pitch competitions.
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: A pitch (text or voice recording), a chosen judge persona, and a difficulty level (Practice / Judge / Investor Mode).
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: A structured founder briefing (problem, solution, users, traction, competitors, ask), multi-round judge questions with feedback, and a final scorecard; optional deal negotiation round.
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Scorecard artifact plus the full Q&A transcript.
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Custom Gradio frontend over Python backend routes; NVIDIA Nemotron powers judge reasoning for pitch structuring, persona-conditioned question generation across rounds, and scorecard analysis, simulating escalating pressure by difficulty level.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Pitch -> face persona-driven follow-up rounds -> optionally negotiate a deal -> read scorecard -> re-pitch to improve score.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It gamifies adversarial rehearsal: the AI's job is to attack the weak points of your argument in persona, and the scorecard makes rhetorical vulnerabilities inspectable and re-testable.

sourceProductCards.name: LLM Cinema
sourceProductCards.concept: A fine-tuned 4B model that writes and 'directs' original ASCII short films: give it a concept and it produces a complete narrative with characters, environments, camera moves, and weather, played back frame-by-frame in a cinema-styled browser UI.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: A one-line film concept in; a ~90-second streamed ASCII animation with narration, staging, and directorial choices out.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Generative video is heavy, slow, and expensive; this delivers watchable, art-directed 'films' from a laptop-class model by radically constraining the render medium.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Creators and AI enthusiasts interested in procedural storytelling and ASCII aesthetics.
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: A creative prompt, or the 'Surprise' button for a random concept.
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: A streamed ASCII animation (~90 seconds) with subtitles/narration, layered scenes, and camera movements.
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Playable frame-by-frame ASCII film in the browser.
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: MiniCPM3-4B with LoRA fine-tuning emits strict JSON describing title, logline, and per-shot staging; a custom ASCII rendering engine resolves character sprites, composes layered scenes with occlusion, terrain textures, and celestial objects, then animates with subtitle streaming. Runs fully offline via llama.cpp exposing an OpenAI-compatible local endpoint, with cloud fallback.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Type or randomize a concept -> watch the film stream shot by shot -> rerun for a different cut.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It splits creativity from rendering: the LLM only outputs structured stage directions (JSON), while a deterministic engine guarantees watchable output — making a 4B model feel like a film studio by shrinking the output medium instead of growing the model.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: 71 likes in the Build Small Hackathon 2026 Thousand Token Wood track; the ASCII-film-from-a-4B-model conceit and cinema-hall UI made it one of the most-liked creative entries.

sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: LLM emits strict structured stage directions; a deterministic domain renderer turns them into rich media — model handles meaning, engine guarantees form.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Shrink the canvas until a tiny model can direct a whole film.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: ASCII as a deliberately low-fidelity, fully controllable render target for generative cinema with camera language.

sourceProductCards.name: Gitopadesh
sourceProductCards.concept: A multilingual spiritual advisor grounded in the Bhagavad Gita: describe a personal dilemma in English, Hindi, or Telugu and receive compassionate guidance in Krishna's persona, citing the specific Sanskrit shloka it draws on, plus a shareable 1080x1080 verse card.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: A personal dilemma in English/Hindi/Telugu in; scripture-cited guidance in persona plus a shareable Devanagari shloka card out.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Generic AI advice has no cultural grounding and scripture study has no personal entry point; this ties an individual's struggle directly to a citable classical verse.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: People facing significant personal or spiritual decisions who value privacy and guidance grounded in classical scripture.
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: A description of a personal problem or dilemma, in English, Hindi, or Telugu.
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: A compassionate persona reply with the relevant shloka (Sanskrit + translation), practical guidance, and a generated 1080x1080 shloka card.
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Shareable square verse card with proper Devanagari rendering plus the cited-guidance text.
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Semantic retrieval (MiniLM embeddings) selects the top 3 relevant verses from a 701-shloka dataset; a Qwen2.5-1.5B model LoRA-fine-tuned on 164 quality-filtered examples generates the persona-consistent, citation-accurate reply; GGUF quantization runs locally via llama.cpp for privacy.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Describe your struggle -> receive verse-grounded counsel -> share or save the shloka card -> return with new dilemmas.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: RAG over a closed classical canon with a persona fine-tune makes hallucination structurally hard (only 701 possible citations) and the shareable verse card turns private counsel into a culturally native social artifact.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Build Small Hackathon 2026 Backyard AI entry with Off-Brand (custom sacred UI), Field Notes, and Best Demo tags; notable as a privacy-first, local-running cultural application among utilitarian entries.

sourceProductCards.name: KrishiSathi
sourceProductCards.concept: A farming companion for small-scale farmers in Nepal: photograph a sick crop, share GPS coordinates, and get a downloadable Report Card combining disease diagnosis, 7-day weather-timed pesticide/fertilizer windows, soil-aware dosages, and irrigation guidance.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: Crop photo + GPS + crop type in; a shareable farm Report Card with diagnosis, timing windows, dosages, and irrigation schedule out.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Rural Nepali farmers lack agricultural consultants; disease identification, weather-aware treatment timing, and soil knowledge are fragmented across sources they cannot access.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Small-scale farmers in rural Nepal without access to agronomists.
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Crop photographs, GPS coordinates, crop type, and field information.
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Disease identification with treatment info plus a Report Card: rainfall forecast, soil nutrient data, recommended actions with timing windows, pesticide/fertilizer dosages, irrigation schedule.
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Downloadable, shareable Report Card document per field/diagnosis.
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: A fine-tuned Vision Transformer (reported 97% accuracy) classifies crop disease from photos; a Qwen 2.5 3B LLM agent orchestrated with LangGraph conditionally routes between Open-Meteo weather API, NARC Soil API (GPS-based soil composition), and DuckDuckGo pest-treatment search to compose grounded recommendations.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Snap sick crop -> confirm crop/field details -> receive diagnosis + timed action plan -> download/share Report Card -> return each season.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It fuses a narrow classifier with public-infrastructure APIs (national soil database, open weather) so the advice is grounded in this field this week, not generic agronomy — and the artifact is designed for sharing in low-connectivity communities.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Cited by name as an example project (agricultural intelligence) in Build Small Hackathon 2026 coverage on the organization page; Backyard AI track entry with local-first and well-tuned tags.

sourceProductCards.name: Exam Panic Rescue
sourceProductCards.concept: Turns last-minute exam panic into a structured rescue plan: upload exam details, weak topics, syllabus photos, and remaining hours, and get a triage timeline, practice drills, confidence analysis, and a printable one-page reference sheet.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: Exam details + syllabus photos + hours left in; a triage study packet with drills, timeline, and a printable cram sheet out.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Panicking students cannot prioritize what to study in limited time; generic study apps assume weeks of runway, not tonight.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Students facing an imminent exam who feel overwhelmed and cannot triage topics.
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Exam details, self-reported weak topics, syllabus photos, and available study time.
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: A rescue packet: drill questions, triage timeline, confidence-pattern analysis, proof targets, review notes, real-time coaching, and a one-page printable summary.
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Printable study packet and one-page reference sheet.
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Three interchangeable small models: MiniCPM-V 4.6 (vision-language, reads syllabus photos and generates content), Nemotron-Mini-4B text alternative, and MiniCPM4 0.5B GGUF on llama.cpp for offline CPU use; models load one at a time on ZeroGPU, with deterministic fallback templates guaranteeing a complete packet even if generation fails.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Dump exam context in a panic -> receive triage plan -> work drills with coaching -> print the one-pager for final review.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: Designed for an emotional emergency moment rather than steady-state studying, and its deterministic fallback templates guarantee the student always gets a usable artifact — reliability engineering aimed at panic.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Build Small Hackathon 2026 Backyard AI entry with Llama Champion, Tiny Titan, Off-Brand, and Field Notes achievements and three sponsor tags, reflecting jury-facing polish.

sourceProductCards.name: Micro RPG Engine
sourceProductCards.concept: A text RPG where a 1-4B model generates the entire world, characters, dialogue, and events in real time, but a Python engine holds authoritative game state: the model proposes changes (HP: -10) and the engine validates and applies them, preventing inconsistency.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: Player text commands in; generated narrative plus engine-validated state updates (HP, inventory, location) out.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Pure-LLM text adventures forget state and let players talk their way into impossible outcomes; pre-written games lack infinite variety. This gets both consistency and novelty.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Players who enjoy AI-driven text adventures and developers exploring small-model game architectures.
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Free-text player commands each turn.
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Narrative descriptions plus structured, validated game-state updates (HP, inventory, location).
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Live game session with inspectable state panel; unique world per playthrough.
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Each turn the Python engine builds context from authoritative game state, sends it to a small model (default Qwen 3 4B Instruct), parses the response into narrative text plus structured mechanics tags, then validates and applies proposed deltas — the model can suggest 'HP: -10' but only the engine mutates numbers. Fine-tuning uses parser-validated synthetic datasets with LoRA.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Type an action -> read generated narrative -> see validated state change -> continue; every run generates a fresh world.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: The propose/validate split (LLM suggests deltas, engine enforces rules) is a general recipe for letting generative models drive systems that must never break invariants.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Build Small Hackathon 2026 Thousand Token Wood entry; its no-pre-written-content, engine-enforced-consistency design earned off-brand and small-model badges in the README.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: 4 likes on the Hugging Face Space (observed 2026-07-08 in org index)

sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Hackathon-scale engine demo on 1-4B models; single maker, mock-mode fallback, no studio.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Generative model proposes structured deltas; a deterministic engine holding authoritative state validates and applies them, so creativity can never corrupt invariants.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: The AI narrates; the engine referees.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: State authority deliberately withheld from the model via a mechanics-tag protocol with parser-validated fine-tuning data.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy 'AI dungeon master' as a raw chat loop where the model tracks (and forgets) state itself.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the text-RPG framing; transfer the propose/validate state protocol to other invariant-bound playful systems (budget adventures, diet quests, classroom simulations).

sourceProductCards.name: Sun Direction (FLUX.2 Klein)
sourceProductCards.concept: Relight your outdoor photo by physically dragging the sun: a 3D ball widget sets the sun's position, and the image is re-rendered with lighting, shadows, and mood matching that sun direction.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: An outdoor photo plus a sun position chosen on a 3D ball in; the same photo relit from that sun direction out.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: You cannot reshoot golden hour; changing the light direction of an existing photo normally requires expert compositing.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Everyday photographers and creators who want a different time-of-day mood for shots they already took.
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: An outdoor photo and a sun direction chosen by rotating a ball-shaped 3D control.
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: The photo re-rendered with lighting and shadows consistent with the selected sun position.
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Relit image for comparison/download.
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: An image-editing diffusion pipeline built on Black Forest Labs' FLUX.2 Klein model, conditioned on a user-specified sun direction taken from an interactive 3D ball control, re-rendering illumination and cast shadows on ZeroGPU hardware.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Upload photo -> drag the sun around the 3D ball -> generate -> compare moods -> download the version you like.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It replaces prompt text with a direct physical metaphor (drag the sun), showing how spatial widgets can parameterize diffusion edits — a UI move, not just a model move.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Surfaced on the Hugging Face Spaces trending front page (observed 2026-07-08) shortly after FLUX.2 Klein's release, as a novel physical-control demo of the new model.

sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Single-maker demo Space on free ZeroGPU showcasing one focused capability; not a product company.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Replace text prompts with a direct-manipulation physical control (drag a sun, slider, dial) whose continuous value conditions the generative edit.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Drag the sun; the photo obeys.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: A 3D spatial widget as the sole conditioning input for photo relighting.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy 'AI photo relighter' with a text prompt box ('make it sunset'), losing the continuous spatial control that makes it feel magical.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy sun-position relighting; transfer continuous physical-widget conditioning to another edit dimension (season dial, weather joystick, age slider for house facades).
