A home-brewing coach tracking fermentation stages with scheduled notifications and mistake triage

coverageGaps: Climate/weather AI: searched but found mostly research model code (ECMWF ai-models, WeatherGFT) without product surfaces; no card included.
Bio/chem/astro lab science beyond literature research (PaperQA2) is not represented by a hands-on product-shaped repo.
Language learning and food are represented only at candidate_only strength (74 and 10 stars); a mid-size product-shaped repo in each remains to be found.
Photo restoration for family archives (DeOldify-class) skipped: dominant repos are pre-2024 or too framework-like; a 2025-2026 riser was not verified.
No agent/devtool/coding cards were included at all (quota of 4 unused), by design given the duplicate-concept problem.
editorNotes: All star counts and release dates were observed directly on repo pages fetched 2026-07-08; approximate ('~') values reflect GitHub's rounded display (e.g. '6.3k').
virattt/ai-hedge-fund was fetched and rejected for size (60.9k stars observed, above the 40k band).
Two music-generation cards (YuE, ACE-Step) are intentionally paired: they encode opposite bets (structural control vs. edit-speed) and should not be merged.
open-notebook and storm sit near the top of the size band (35.1k and 29.9k observed) — treat their patterns, not their positioning, as the reusable material.

Domains represented: music generation, personal health, audiobooks/accessibility, dictation/accessibility, humanitarian mapping, home automation, video localization, digital painting, sports analytics, scientific literature, podcast generation, consumer photo compliance, interactive fiction, personal finance, research notebooks, knowledge writing, language learning (candidate), cooking (candidate) — 18 domains across 19 cards.
Exclusion list was checked: no card overlaps the already-indexed repos or signals.

sourceProductCards.name: Nutshell: Hands-Free Web Access for Everyone
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: hands-free web browsing for people with motor disabilities
sourceProductCards.concept: A Chrome extension that lets people with motor disabilities browse the web entirely hands-free using webcam head tracking and mouth gestures, with on-device Gemini Nano summarizing pages and videos so less navigation is needed in the first place.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: User moves their head and opens their mouth in front of a webcam; the browser cursor moves, clicks, scrolls, and long pages/videos get AI summaries.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: People who cannot operate a mouse or keyboard are locked out of ordinary web browsing, and assistive hardware is expensive; reading long pages is also physically costly when every scroll is effortful.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: People with motor disabilities who cannot use traditional input devices
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Webcam video of the user's head pose and mouth gestures, plus a 5-point calibration
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Hands-free cursor control (move, click, scroll, back/forward) plus AI-generated summaries of articles and YouTube videos
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Live controllable browser session with on-page summary panels and visual dwell/click feedback rings
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Human.js computer vision tracks head translation/rotation from the webcam; One-Euro filtering smooths it into cursor motion within a personalized calibrated control space; dwell timers arm clickable elements and a mouth-open gesture fires the click; Chrome's built-in Summarizer API condenses articles and the Prompt API summarizes YouTube captions extracted via XHR interception - all on-device.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Calibrate once with 5 head positions, then continuously browse: hover by head movement, click by opening mouth, request summaries when a page is too long to physically scroll through.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It pairs an input-replacement layer (head/mouth as mouse) with an effort-reduction layer (AI summaries reduce how much navigation is needed at all) - treating accessibility as minimizing total motor cost, not just remapping inputs.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Named Most Helpful winner (Chrome Extension) of the Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025, selected from 1,300+ submissions per Google's official winners blog.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: Winner, Most Helpful Application (Chrome Extension), Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025
Featured in Google's official Chrome for Developers winners announcement blog
attentionProof: Winner, Most Helpful Application (Chrome Extension), Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025
Featured in Google's official Chrome for Developers winners announcement blog
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Demonstrates the pattern of combining a novel low-cost input modality with AI content compression so the assistive layer reduces both physical and cognitive load simultaneously.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Single-developer hackathon Chrome extension announced November 2025; no company or distribution beyond the challenge.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Replace an inaccessible input channel with a cheap sensor-based proxy, then use on-device AI to shrink the amount of interaction the task requires at all.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Accessibility = remap the input AND compress the task.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Dwell+gesture clicking fused with on-device summarization so users navigate less, not just differently.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy the head-tracking cursor Chrome extension verbatim.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the head-tracked browser cursor; transfer the 'cheap-sensor input proxy + AI task compression' pairing to another constrained-interaction domain (e.g. cooking with dirty hands, driving, bedridden device control).
bestRemixTargets: Hands-busy kitchen assistant that navigates recipes via head gestures and summarizes steps aloud
Bedside hospital entertainment controller driven by facial gestures with AI-condensed content
Workshop/maker assistant for gloved hands that turns manuals into gesture-navigable step cards

sourceProductCards.name: AAC Board AI
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: augmentative and alternative communication (AAC) for people with speech disabilities
sourceProductCards.concept: A web AAC board where users with speech or language disabilities (ALS, autism, cerebral palsy) compose messages by tapping pictograms, and built-in browser AI turns the telegraphic pictogram string into fluent, tone-adjusted, translatable speech.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: User taps pictograms on a communication board; out comes a grammatically complete, tone-matched sentence spoken aloud in the chosen language.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: AAC boards produce robotic telegraphic output ('I want go grandma') that flattens the speaker's personality and doesn't translate; polishing every utterance manually is slow for people for whom each interaction is already effortful.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: People with speech disabilities and the speech therapists who configure their boards
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Sequences of pictogram taps forming a raw message, plus a chosen tone and output language
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: A refined natural-language sentence, tone-adjusted and optionally translated, spoken via text-to-speech
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Spoken sentence plus the visible corrected text on the AAC board interface
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Pictogram selections build a telegraphic draft; Chrome's Proofreader API expands it into grammatical English ('I want to go to Grandma's'), the Rewriter API restyles it to a selected tone (formal/casual/playful), and the Translator API converts it into any supported TTS language - all running client-side with Open Board Format compatibility and IndexedDB persistence.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Tap pictograms, preview the AI-polished sentence, pick tone, hit speak; therapists configure boards once and users communicate repeatedly.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It treats grammatical repair, tone, and translation as a post-processing chain on top of an existing standardized input format (Open Board Format), giving expressive range back to users whose input bandwidth is fixed.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Named Most Helpful winner (Web Application) of the Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025 in Google's official winners announcement.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: Winner, Most Helpful Application (Web Application), Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025
Featured in Google's official Chrome for Developers winners announcement blog
attentionProof: Winner, Most Helpful Application (Web Application), Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025
Featured in Google's official Chrome for Developers winners announcement blog
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Clean example of layering AI repair/restyle/translate stages onto a constrained symbolic input system - a structure reusable wherever input bandwidth is limited but expressive output matters.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Hackathon-built web app announced November 2025; no commercial AAC vendor behind it.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Take a low-bandwidth symbolic input stream and run it through a fix-grammar, then match-tone, then translate AI pipeline to restore expressive personality.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Symbols in, personality out.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Tone control and translation for AAC users, not just word prediction - personality as a first-class output dimension.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would rebuild a pictogram board with an LLM polish button.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the AAC pictogram board; transfer the 'constrained-input to expressive-output repair chain' to another low-bandwidth channel (e.g. morse/switch input, emoji-only messaging, template-based reporting).
bestRemixTargets: Emoji-string-to-diplomatic-email composer for aphasia or low-literacy users
Switch-scanning input for locked-in patients with AI sentence expansion and tone presets
Kids' picture-based diary app that turns icon sequences into readable family updates in grandparents' language

sourceProductCards.name: Phonaify
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: English pronunciation coaching embedded in everyday web reading
sourceProductCards.concept: A Chrome extension for English learners: highlight any word on any webpage, hear its correct pronunciation, record your own attempt, and get instant phonetic feedback from on-device AI without leaving the page.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: User highlights a word and speaks it; the extension returns correct audio plus a phoneme-level diff of what they got wrong.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Pronunciation practice is siloed in dedicated apps, so learners never practice on the words they actually encounter while reading; existing browser language tools focus on grammar and vocabulary, not speech.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: English language learners with speaking anxiety who read English content online daily
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: A highlighted word on any webpage plus a microphone recording of the learner saying it
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Reference pronunciation audio, phonetic analysis of the attempt, and personalized corrective feedback
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Inline feedback popup showing phoneme comparison and accuracy guidance
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Text selection triggers reference pronunciation; the learner's recording is analyzed by the on-device Gemini Nano model, and a Longest Common Subsequence alignment between expected and produced phonetic sequences localizes exactly which sounds diverged, producing targeted feedback without any server round-trip.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Read normally, hover/highlight an unfamiliar word, listen-record-compare in a few seconds, continue reading - micro-practice loops embedded in existing habits.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It converts an existing passive habit (reading the web) into distributed pronunciation training, and uses a classic string alignment algorithm on phoneme sequences to make LLM speech feedback precise rather than vague.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Winner of Best Multimodal AI Application (Chrome Extension) at the Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025, highlighted in Google's official winners blog.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: Winner, Best Multimodal AI Application (Chrome Extension), Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025
Published on the Chrome Web Store per its Devpost page
attentionProof: Winner, Best Multimodal AI Application (Chrome Extension), Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025
Published on the Chrome Web Store per its Devpost page
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Shows the 'practice embedded in an existing habit surface' pattern plus algorithmic grounding (LCS on phonemes) that makes generative feedback verifiably specific.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Solo hackathon extension from late 2025; no language-learning company behind it.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Embed micro-practice into the surface where the need is encountered, and align model output against a symbolic reference to localize the user's exact error.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Practice where you already are; diff the attempt against the reference.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Phoneme-level LCS diffing turns fuzzy 'say it better' feedback into a pinpointed correction, fully on-device.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy the highlight-a-word pronunciation checker extension.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the pronunciation Chrome extension; transfer the 'attempt-vs-reference symbolic diff' loop to another skill practiced in situ (sight-reading music, sign language, code review phrasing).
bestRemixTargets: Sheet-music reader that listens to you play a highlighted bar and diffs your notes against the score
Karaoke-style dialect coach that diffs your intonation against regional reference audio on streaming subtitles
Medical terminology pronunciation trainer embedded in nursing-school reading materials

sourceProductCards.name: Pet Shelter App
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: animal shelter adoption operations and pet profile marketing
sourceProductCards.concept: A platform that lets under-resourced animal shelters turn a pet photo and a few staff notes into a polished, translated, filterable adoption profile using free browser-based AI instead of paid cloud services.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: Shelter staff upload a pet photo and rough notes; out comes a professionally written, proofread, multi-language adoption profile in a public gallery.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Small shelters lack the time, writing skill, and budget to produce compelling adoption listings, so adoptable pets sit unseen; cloud AI services cost money they don't have.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Under-resourced animal shelters and rescue organizations
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: A pet photo plus brief descriptive notes from shelter staff
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: A complete adoption profile: identified breed/age, suggested name, engaging description, proofread and translated into English/Spanish/French
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Published pet profile pages in a filterable public web gallery
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Chrome's Prompt API analyzes the uploaded photo to identify animal type, breed, estimated age and suggest a name; the Writer API expands staff shorthand into an engaging adoption narrative; the Proofreader API cleans grammar; the Translator API produces Spanish and French versions - a four-stage built-in-AI pipeline with zero API cost.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Staff batch-process intake: photo + notes per animal, review the generated profile, publish; adopters browse and filter the gallery in their language.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It targets an operational bottleneck (listing quality) rather than the matching problem everyone else attacks, and its zero-marginal-cost AI pipeline is itself the product thesis for cash-poor nonprofits.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Winner of Best Multimodal AI Application (Web Application) at the Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025 per Google's official winners blog.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: Winner, Best Multimodal AI Application (Web Application), Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025
Live deployment at petshelter.app referenced on its Devpost page
attentionProof: Winner, Best Multimodal AI Application (Web Application), Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025
Live deployment at petshelter.app referenced on its Devpost page
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Exemplifies 'photo + shorthand notes to publication-ready marketing asset' pipelines for resource-poor organizations - a widely transferable operational-AI shape.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Hackathon-built free tool from late 2025 by an individual developer; not a funded pet-tech company.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Chain vision-identify, write-expand, proofread, translate stages to convert frontline workers' raw capture into publishable multilingual content at zero marginal cost.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Turn intake snapshots into marketing assets automatically.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Free on-device AI as the explicit affordability strategy for nonprofits, not just a privacy feature.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would rebuild a pet adoption listing generator.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the pet shelter gallery; transfer the 'raw field capture to polished multilingual listing' pipeline to another under-resourced sector (food banks, community libraries, secondhand charity shops).
bestRemixTargets: Charity shop donation-to-listing tool that photographs items and generates priced, translated online listings
Community garden surplus board that turns harvest photos into pickup listings with recipes
Lost-and-found municipal portal that writes searchable multilingual descriptions from photos

sourceProductCards.name: Trail
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: offline trail navigation and outdoor safety for hikers
sourceProductCards.concept: A Progressive Web App hiking companion that pre-builds an AI knowledge base about your route while online, then answers questions about flora, fauna, first aid, and navigation fully offline on the trail using Chrome's local Gemini Nano.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: User plans a route online; on the trail with no signal, they get an offline map with GPS position plus a local AI that answers safety and nature questions.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Trails are exactly where connectivity dies, so cloud AI assistants and online maps fail when hikers most need identification, first-aid, and navigation help.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Hikers, trail runners, and cyclists exploring areas with poor cell coverage
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Start/end points, trip type and difficulty during planning; questions and photo uploads during the hike
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Offline interactive route map with position tracking plus instant local-AI answers about local conditions, wildlife, and first aid
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Installable PWA with cached MapLibre map tiles, route data, and a chat that works in airplane mode
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Two-phase architecture: online, Google Maps APIs generate the route and landmarks while the cloud Gemini API compiles a location-specific knowledge base (conditions, hazards, species, safety) cached via Service Workers/IndexedDB; offline, Chrome's built-in Gemini Nano answers questions locally against that cache while MapLibre renders the stored map with GPS positioning.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Plan at home, sync, then hike: glance at offline map for position, ask the local AI when encountering a plant, animal, or injury.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It splits AI work across a connectivity boundary - heavyweight knowledge compilation online, lightweight local inference offline - making 'AI that anticipates being disconnected' the core design move.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Winner of Best Hybrid AI Application (Web Application) at the Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025 per Google's official winners blog.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: Winner, Best Hybrid AI Application (Web Application), Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025
Live site trailplanner101.com referenced on its Devpost page
attentionProof: Winner, Best Hybrid AI Application (Web Application), Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025
Live site trailplanner101.com referenced on its Devpost page
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Canonical example of the 'pre-compile knowledge online, infer locally offline' hybrid pattern - reusable anywhere users predictably lose connectivity.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Hackathon PWA from November 2025; not affiliated with AllTrails/Komoot-scale companies.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Anticipate disconnection: compile a scoped knowledge base with cloud AI ahead of time, then serve it through an on-device model when offline.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Cloud prepares, edge answers.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Treats the connectivity boundary as an architectural seam for AI workloads rather than a failure mode.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy the offline hiking-map-plus-chatbot PWA.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the trail navigation app; transfer the 'pre-compiled scoped knowledge + offline local inference' split to other disconnected contexts (flights, ferries, disaster zones, rural fieldwork).
remixableThemes: safety information at point of need
bestRemixTargets: Long-haul flight companion that pre-compiles destination knowledge and answers offline during the flight
Disaster-preparedness kit app with pre-cached local emergency knowledge served by on-device AI when networks fail
Field-vet assistant for rural livestock visits with region-scoped offline diagnosis support

sourceProductCards.name: EduAdapt
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: neurodivergent-friendly reading adaptation for ADHD and dyslexic schoolchildren
sourceProductCards.concept: A Chrome extension that rewrites any webpage into a reading experience tailored to a specific child's grade level and neurotype (ADHD or dyslexia), producing simplified text, summaries, and task checklists on-device in seconds.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: Teacher/parent sets a student profile (grade, diagnosis, support level); any webpage the child opens is reformatted into simplified, structured, checklist-supported reading.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Web content is written for one generic reader; neurodivergent students need individually adapted text, and teachers cannot manually rewrite every page for every child.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Elementary students with ADHD or dyslexia, and educators who need scalable accessible materials
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: A webpage plus a stored student profile: grade level, diagnosis type, and support level
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: The same page rewritten with simplified language, visual adjustments, quick-scan summaries, and step-by-step task checklists
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Reformatted live webpage with cached adapted versions for repeat visits
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Content scripts extract page structure; the on-device Gemini Nano Prompt API rewrites text conditioned on the child's grade/diagnosis profile while the Summarizer API produces quick scans; responses are cached for speed, and everything runs client-side under Manifest V3 so children's data never leaves the device.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: One-time profile setup, then invisible ongoing adaptation: the child browses normally and every page arrives pre-adapted; teachers adjust support levels as skills grow.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It parameterizes accessibility by an individual learner profile (grade x diagnosis x support level) instead of offering generic 'simplify' buttons - personalization as a persistent lens over the whole web.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Honorable mention in the Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025, called out by name in Google's official winners blog among 1,300+ submissions.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: Honorable Mention, Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025
Named in Google's official Chrome for Developers winners announcement blog
attentionProof: Honorable Mention, Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025
Named in Google's official Chrome for Developers winners announcement blog
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Shows the 'persistent personal lens' pattern: a stored learner profile silently reshaping all content, with privacy-by-on-device as an enabler for child users.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Individual developer's hackathon extension from late 2025; no edtech company distribution.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Store a rich user-condition profile once, then apply it as an automatic AI transformation lens over every piece of content the user encounters.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Profile once, adapt everything.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Diagnosis-and-grade-conditioned rewriting cached per page, rather than one-off simplification on demand.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy the ADHD/dyslexia webpage simplifier extension.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the neurodivergent reading extension; transfer the 'stored condition profile as an always-on content lens' to another audience (low-vision recipe adaptation, ESL work documents, senior-friendly government forms).
bestRemixTargets: Government-form translator that adapts bureaucratic pages to a stored literacy/language profile
Recipe site lens that rewrites any recipe to a cook's stored skill level and kitchen equipment
Workplace-onboarding lens that adapts internal docs to a new hire's stored role and background

sourceProductCards.name: The Turing Werewolf
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: solo social deduction gaming with AI-simulated players
sourceProductCards.concept: A fully client-side Werewolf game where a single human plays villager, werewolf, or fool against a room of Chrome built-in AI agents that debate, accuse, deceive, and vote like a real party - no server, no other humans needed.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: User joins a Werewolf lobby of local AI players and types accusations/defenses; the AI villagers argue back, form suspicions, and vote to eliminate suspects.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Social deduction games require gathering 6+ people; solo players have no way to practice or enjoy the genre, and cloud-LLM NPC games cost money per session.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Solo players who love social deduction games but lack a group
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Free-text discussion messages, secret votes, and role actions during turn-based rounds
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Turn-by-turn NPC speeches, accusations, and vote outcomes advancing a full Werewolf match
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Playable in-browser game session with visible discussion log and elimination results
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Each NPC runs its own cloned Chrome Prompt API session with distinct reasoning; NPC outputs are JSON-structured into public speech plus hidden logic, validated by schema, with XState managing game phases; a 'style normalizer' uses the Rewriter API to strip the human player's writing quirks so AI agents cannot fingerprint them - all inference local.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Round loop: read AI table talk, contribute arguments or lies, vote secretly, survive to the next night phase.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: Separating each NPC's public utterance from hidden JSON reasoning creates genuinely deceptive agents, and the anti-fingerprinting rewriter is a rare 'protect the human from the AI's perception' mechanic.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Honorable mention in the Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025, named in Google's official winners blog as a standout solo social deduction experience.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: Honorable Mention, Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025
Named in Google's official Chrome for Developers winners announcement blog
attentionProof: Honorable Mention, Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025
Named in Google's official Chrome for Developers winners announcement blog
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Demonstrates multi-agent theater with public/private cognition separation and human-anonymization mechanics - fertile structure for any 'crowd simulation' product.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Solo developer's free browser game from late 2025; no studio or monetization.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Give each AI agent a hidden reasoning channel separate from its public output, and offer the human a style-anonymization layer so the simulated crowd cannot identify them.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: A crowd of locally-run liars, and a mask for the human among them.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Anti-fingerprinting rewriting as a gameplay mechanic - the AI is smart enough that the human needs camouflage.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would rebuild AI Werewolf with LLM players.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the Werewolf game; transfer the 'simulated deliberating crowd with hidden reasoning' structure to other formats (mock jury practice for law students, negotiation training, panel-interview rehearsal).
bestRemixTargets: Mock jury deliberation simulator where law students argue against AI jurors with hidden biases
Salary negotiation dojo against a panel of AI stakeholders with private agendas
Language-learning debate club where AI classmates argue in the target language at your level

sourceProductCards.name: HawkWatch
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: intelligent video surveillance for small-business security and medical emergencies
sourceProductCards.concept: An AI surveillance platform that watches many camera feeds simultaneously, detects crimes (shoplifting, assault) and medical emergencies (fainting, choking), and sends timestamped, evidence-backed alerts that staff can dismiss or escalate.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: User connects live camera streams or MP4s; the system returns real-time alerts with incident timelines, security reports, and CSV exports when it detects crime or medical distress.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Security personnel cannot genuinely monitor multiple feeds at once, so incidents are recorded but missed in the moment; small businesses cannot afford human monitoring at all.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Security teams at grocery stores, malls, hospitals, and small local businesses
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Live camera streams or uploaded MP4 video files
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Real-time email/phone alerts, incident timelines with evidence, analysis reports, and CSV data exports
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Alert dashboard monitoring multiple streams with dismiss/report triage actions and generated incident reports
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Video frames flow through Google's Gemini visual language model and TensorFlow.js processing to classify events spanning criminal activity to health emergencies, combining audio, video, and body-position signals; detections generate timestamped reports with captured evidence and trigger Resend-based notifications, with a human triage step (dismiss or escalate) in the loop.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Connect feeds once; staff live in the triage dashboard, confirming or dismissing AI-raised incidents rather than watching raw video.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It reframes surveillance from 'record everything' to 'triage queue of narrated incidents,' using a VLM's open-vocabulary understanding to cover both crime and medical distress in one system.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Grand Prize winner ($11,000, 1st place) at TreeHacks 2025, Stanford's flagship hackathon, as reported by The Stanford Daily and its Devpost page.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: Grand Prize, 1st Place ($11,000), TreeHacks 2025 at Stanford
Covered by The Stanford Daily's TreeHacks 2025 report
attentionProof: Grand Prize, 1st Place ($11,000), TreeHacks 2025 at Stanford
Covered by The Stanford Daily's TreeHacks 2025 report
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Strong 'continuous sensor stream to human triage queue' pattern with open-vocabulary VLM classification - transferable far beyond security.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Student team's 36-hour hackathon build from February 2025; not a funded security vendor.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Run a VLM over continuous video to convert passive footage into a prioritized, evidence-attached triage queue that one human can supervise.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Cameras that raise their hands instead of just recording.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: One open-vocabulary model covering both crime and medical emergencies, with dismissal/escalation as the primary UI.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy the AI security-camera alert dashboard.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the crime-surveillance product; transfer the 'continuous feed to narrated triage queue' pattern to other passive streams (kiln/workshop safety, pool lifeguarding, wildlife camera traps, kitchen hygiene).
bestRemixTargets: Pool/beach lifeguard assistant that flags drowning-pattern motion across feeds
Elder-care home fall-and-distress monitor with dignity-preserving on-premise processing
Wildlife camera-trap triage that narrates and prioritizes rare-species sightings for rangers

sourceProductCards.name: FridgeFit
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: fitness-goal-aligned meal planning from fridge photos
sourceProductCards.concept: An app that turns a single photo of your open fridge into three complete meal plans matched to your fitness goal (bulking, cutting, clean eating), each with recipes and macro breakdowns, using only ingredients you already have.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: User photographs their fridge and picks a fitness goal; out come three meal options with recipes and calorie/protein/carb/fat macros.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: The nightly 'what can I even make?' decision paralysis, plus the gap between owning ingredients and knowing which combination serves a training goal - which also drives food waste.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Fitness-conscious people who meal-plan around training goals and want to use what they have
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: A photo of refrigerator contents plus a selected fitness objective
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Three tailored meal plans with recipes and full macro breakdowns
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Meal cards with recipe steps and macro tables in the web app
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: GPT-4o Vision identifies the individual ingredients visible in the fridge photo; a generation step then composes three candidate meals constrained to those ingredients and conditioned on the chosen fitness goal, computing per-meal macro breakdowns, served via Supabase-authenticated Edge Functions.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Snap fridge, tap goal, pick one of three meals, cook; repeat whenever the fridge state changes.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It fuses inventory sensing (vision) with goal-conditioned generation, collapsing three separate chores - inventory check, recipe search, macro math - into one photo.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Honorable Mention winner at PhysTech 2025 (Physical Activity and Technology Hack Day), listed among winners in the official Devpost gallery.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: Honorable Mention Winner, PhysTech 2025 (Physical Activity and Technology Hack Day)
Listed in the PhysTech 2025 Devpost winners gallery
attentionProof: Honorable Mention Winner, PhysTech 2025 (Physical Activity and Technology Hack Day)
Listed in the PhysTech 2025 Devpost winners gallery
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Clean 'photograph your inventory, get goal-optimized plans' pattern applicable to any resource-to-plan domain.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Three-person hackathon build from June 2025 hosted on Netlify; no company behind it.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Use vision to inventory what the user physically has, then generate plans optimized for a declared goal strictly within that inventory.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Photo of what you have + goal = plan you can execute now.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Constraining generation to visually-verified on-hand resources kills the 'recipe requires shopping' failure mode.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy the fridge-photo recipe generator.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the fridge-to-meal app; transfer the 'vision inventory + goal-constrained plan' move to other resource domains (closet-to-outfit for a dress code, toolbox-to-repair-plan, craft-stash-to-project).
bestRemixTargets: Closet-photo stylist that assembles outfits for an occasion from clothes you own
Garage-photo repair planner that matches home fixes to tools and materials on hand
Yarn-stash knitting planner generating patterns sized to your exact stash

sourceProductCards.name: Basketball Shot Analyzer
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: basketball jump-shot technique coaching from self-recorded video
sourceProductCards.concept: A web app where basketball players upload videos of their jump shots and get phase-by-phase technique feedback (beginning, release, follow-through), with every session stored so improvement trends are tracked across submissions over time.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: User uploads a jump-shot video; out comes frame-level pose-based feedback on each shot phase plus longitudinal trend charts across all past uploads.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Players without access to a coach get no objective feedback on shooting mechanics, and one-off analysis apps don't show whether practice is actually changing anything.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Basketball players without personal coaching who practice alone
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Video files of the player's basketball shots
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Technique feedback per shot phase and historical trend analysis across multiple videos
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Feedback report per video plus a Kintone-backed trend dashboard over time
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: MediaPipe pose estimation extracts body keypoints frame-by-frame while OpenCV handles video processing with intelligent frame skipping for speed; scene-change detection via frame differencing suppresses unreliable pose reads during cuts; per-phase joint analysis produces feedback, and results persist to Kintone so trends accumulate across sessions.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Record practice, upload, read phase feedback, adjust form, upload again next session - a longitudinal self-coaching loop.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: The differentiator is longitudinal storage: it treats each analysis as a data point in a coaching timeline rather than a one-shot verdict, and it engineers around video-quality failure modes (scene changes) that break naive pose pipelines.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Honorable Mention and Kintone Integration Challenge Award winner at PhysTech 2025, listed in the official Devpost winners gallery.
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Represents the 'self-recorded video to phase-segmented technique feedback with longitudinal tracking' pattern - the backbone of any solo skill-coaching product.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Solo hackathon web app from June 2025; no sports-tech company involved.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Segment a repeated physical action into named phases via pose estimation, score each phase, and persist scores so the user sees a trendline instead of isolated verdicts.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Phase-segmented pose feedback plus a memory of every attempt.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Trend accumulation across uploads turns a gimmick analyzer into a coaching relationship.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy the basketball shot form checker.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the jump-shot analyzer; transfer the 'phase-segmented pose scoring with a stored trendline' loop to other repeated motions (golf swing, physiotherapy exercises, violin bowing, weightlifting form).
bestRemixTargets: Physio homework companion verifying rehab exercise form and charting recovery week over week
Golf swing phase analyzer with longitudinal handicap-correlated trends
Dance practice mirror that scores choreography sections across rehearsal sessions

sourceProductCards.name: CarbonCompass
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: sustainability-scored business location siting for entrepreneurs
sourceProductCards.concept: A tool where an entrepreneur describes a business idea in one sentence ('eco cafe') and gets 2-3 recommended neighborhoods on an interactive 3D map, each color-coded by a composite Green Score balancing profitability factors with environmental impact.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: User types a business concept into chat; out comes a ranked, color-coded 3D map of neighborhoods with sustainability scores and rationale.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Location decisions lock in a business's energy costs, emissions, and foot traffic for years, but small entrepreneurs have no way to weigh environmental siting factors alongside commercial ones.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Entrepreneurs and small business founders choosing where to open a physical location
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: A natural-language description of the planned business, plus optional photos/videos of candidate spaces
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: 2-3 ranked neighborhood recommendations with Green Scores and explanatory rationale on an interactive Mapbox 3D map
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Interactive 3D map with color-coded neighborhood scores and per-recommendation explanations
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Google's Gemini API interprets the business concept; location data on energy efficiency, waste generation, walkability, transit access, safety, and zoning is fused into a composite Green Score rendered on Mapbox GL 3D tiles via Cloudflare Workers/D1/R2; uploaded photos or videos of candidate spaces are converted into 3D models via Gaussian splatting with lidar, and infrared overlays surface energy leaks.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Describe the business, explore scored neighborhoods on the map, then upload a walkthrough of a shortlisted space for a 3D energy inspection.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It moves climate tooling from reporting past emissions to steering an upstream decision (where to site a business), and layers consumer-grade 3D capture (Gaussian splatting) into what is otherwise a scoring product.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: First place in the 3D Vision for Climate: Coolant's Sustainability Challenge track at HackHarvard 2025 (500+ students), covered by ASU's engineering school news.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: 1st Place, Coolant Climate Tech / 3D Vision for Climate Sustainability Challenge Track, HackHarvard 2025
Covered by Arizona State University SCAI news article
attentionProof: 1st Place, Coolant Climate Tech / 3D Vision for Climate Sustainability Challenge Track, HackHarvard 2025
Covered by Arizona State University SCAI news article
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Demonstrates 'score an irreversible upstream decision on a map' plus multi-signal composite scoring - a reusable geospatial decision-support shape.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Three undergraduates' 36-hour hackathon build from October 2025; no company or dataset partnerships.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Fuse heterogeneous public geodata into one composite score per map cell, conditioned on the user's stated intent, and explain each recommendation.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Intent-conditioned map scoring for a decision you only make once.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Sustainability as a siting input rather than a reporting output, with 3D capture for site-level verification.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy the green business-location picker map.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the business-siting product; transfer 'intent-conditioned composite map scoring' to other one-shot location choices (where to retire, school catchment choice, community garden placement, EV charger siting).
bestRemixTargets: Retirement-town chooser scoring healthcare access, climate risk, and cost against a personal profile
Community solar/garden siting tool for neighborhood associations
Film-location scout ranking neighborhoods by permit friction, light, and noise data

sourceProductCards.name: FaceTimeOS
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: voice-controlled remote computer use over consumer video calling
sourceProductCards.concept: A system that turns FaceTime into a remote-control channel for your Mac: call your own computer from anywhere, speak a task over the call while watching via screen share, and a computer-use agent executes it on the desktop.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: User FaceTime-calls their Mac and says what they need done; the agent clicks and types through the task while narrating progress back over the call.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Remote desktop tools require setup, ports, and a second computer; when you're away from your Mac and something urgent needs doing, there is no zero-install, phone-native way to operate it.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Anyone who needs to operate their own computer while away from it, starting from just their phone
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Voice commands spoken over a FaceTime audio call to the user's own Mac
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Executed desktop actions visible via FaceTime screen share, with spoken progress narration
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Live screen-share session showing the agent completing the requested task
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: A virtual audio device captures FaceTime call audio and pipes it into speech recognition; the transcribed intent drives Agent S (Simular), a computer-use agent performing UI grounding and CUA tool calls; in parallel a Fetch.ai agent converts the action trajectory into natural-speech status updates played back into the call - three microservices (computer-use, UI, backend) behind REST interfaces.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Call the Mac, state a task, watch the shared screen as it executes, interject corrections by voice, hang up when done.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It repurposes a ubiquitous consumer channel (FaceTime) as both the input bus and the observability layer for a computer-use agent - no client install, and the human watches every action live.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: 1st Place Grand Prize at Cal Hacks 12.0 out of 695 submitted projects from 3,000+ hackers, per the winning team's writeup.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: 1st Place Grand Prize, Cal Hacks 12.0 (695 submissions, 3,000+ hackers)
Detailed technical writeup published by the winning team
attentionProof: 1st Place Grand Prize, Cal Hacks 12.0 (695 submissions, 3,000+ hackers)
Detailed technical writeup published by the winning team
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: The 'existing consumer channel as agent I/O plus live observability' move is the learning value - distinct from generic computer-use demos.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Student weekend build from Cal Hacks 12.0 (2025); open-source repo, no product distribution.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Route agent commands and live visual feedback through a channel the user already has (call, chat, screen share), making trust-by-watching the default interface.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Call your computer like a person and watch it work.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: FaceTime as simultaneously the command channel, auth boundary, and audit trail for agentic control.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy the voice-controlled Mac agent over FaceTime.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the FaceTime-Mac agent; transfer 'familiar channel as agent I/O with watch-it-work trust' to non-desktop targets (call your home greenhouse controller, voice-drive a 3D printer farm via video call).
bestRemixTargets: Grandparent tech-support line where family calls a relative's computer and an agent fixes issues while both watch
Video-call kiln/workshop supervisor that executes machine adjustments while streaming the result
Farm equipment check-in: call the barn camera system and voice-command irrigation changes with visual confirmation

sourceProductCards.name: EcoBite
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: household food waste measurement and behavior change
sourceProductCards.concept: A mobile app that lets people photograph their food waste; image analysis estimates the weight of what's being thrown away, turning an invisible habit into tracked numbers that nudge more sustainable consumption.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: User photographs food they are discarding; the app estimates waste weight and accumulates a personal waste-tracking record.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Food waste (about 8% of global greenhouse emissions per EPA figures cited by the team) is invisible at the household level - nobody weighs their scraps, so there is no feedback loop to change behavior.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Environmentally-minded individuals and households wanting to see and shrink their food waste
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Photos of food being wasted or discarded
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Estimated waste weight per photo and cumulative tracking of personal waste over time
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: In-app waste log with per-item weight estimates and trends
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Uploaded food photos run through image analysis that identifies the food and estimates the mass being discarded; estimates accumulate into a personal waste ledger designed to raise awareness of consumption patterns - built rapidly in FlutterFlow with mentoring from FlutterFlow staff at the event.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Photograph scraps before binning them, review weekly waste totals, adjust shopping and cooking habits accordingly.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It applies 'quantified self' mechanics to trash: vision-based mass estimation converts a photo into a metric, creating a feedback loop where none existed.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: First place in the Best Use of FlutterFlow category at Stanford TreeHacks 2025 (1,000+ students), covered by Duke Kunshan University's Humanities Research Center.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: 1st Place, Best Use of FlutterFlow, TreeHacks 2025 at Stanford
Covered by Duke Kunshan University HRC news article
attentionProof: 1st Place, Best Use of FlutterFlow, TreeHacks 2025 at Stanford
Covered by Duke Kunshan University HRC news article
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Represents 'photo to physical-quantity estimate to behavioral feedback loop' - a compact, transferable measurement-product shape.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Student hackathon app from February 2025; no startup or app-store distribution observed.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Estimate a physical quantity from a casual photo and feed it into a personal ledger that makes an invisible behavior measurable.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Photograph the thing you throw away; get a number that changes behavior.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Mass estimation from waste photos as the entire product - measurement, not recipes, as the intervention.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy the food-waste photo tracker.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the food waste tracker; transfer 'casual-photo physical quantification with a behavior ledger' to other invisible flows (water use from meter photos, closet dead-stock, packaging waste, leftover paint).
bestRemixTargets: Restaurant kitchen prep-waste dashboard from end-of-shift photos
Wardrobe utilization tracker photographing what you actually wear vs own
Household packaging-waste scorer that grades your weekly recycling photo

sourceProductCards.name: HiveMind
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: real-time comprehension monitoring and peer grouping in virtual classrooms
sourceProductCards.concept: A collaborative AI platform that plugs into Zoom classes, continuously assesses which students are lost using quizzes, transcriptions, and AI analysis, and automatically regroups struggling students into peer-learning 'nodes' matched to their needs.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: Class runs on Zoom as usual; the system ingests quizzes and transcripts, flags who is falling behind, and outputs dynamically matched peer support groups.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: In virtual classrooms teachers cannot read the room; struggling students stay silent and generic breakout rooms ignore who actually needs what kind of help.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Instructors and students in virtual/hybrid classrooms
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Live Zoom class signals: quiz responses, meeting transcriptions, and engagement data
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Real-time comprehension assessments and automatic assignment of students into tailored peer-learning nodes
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Dashboard of per-student understanding plus generated peer-group assignments
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Zoom integration streams class activity; quiz results and transcriptions are analyzed with OpenAI and Perplexity APIs (with agent components) to estimate each student's real-time understanding; a grouping step then clusters students into complementary peer-learning nodes so stronger peers support weaker ones on the specific misunderstood material.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Teacher teaches normally; the system surfaces who is lost and reshuffles peer nodes; students get help from matched classmates instead of raising hands.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It uses AI not to tutor directly but to orchestrate humans - the model's output is a social structure (who should help whom on what), a distinctive 'AI as matchmaker' pattern.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Grand Prize in Education at Stanford TreeHacks 2025 ($1,000 plus Meta Ray-Ban Smart Glasses), covered by NIU College of Business news and diaspora press.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: Grand Prize in Education, TreeHacks 2025 at Stanford ($1,000 + Meta Ray-Ban Smart Glasses)
Covered by NIU College of Business news and New India Abroad
attentionProof: Grand Prize in Education, TreeHacks 2025 at Stanford ($1,000 + Meta Ray-Ban Smart Glasses)
Covered by NIU College of Business news and New India Abroad
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: The 'AI assesses individuals, then outputs an optimal human-to-human pairing' structure is a reusable alternative to AI-as-tutor.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Graduate student's TreeHacks 2025 project; no edtech company or deployment observed.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Continuously score individuals from ambient activity signals, then let the AI's primary output be matching people to each other rather than answering directly.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: AI as the invisible matchmaker, humans as the tutors.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Peer-node regrouping driven by live comprehension estimates - the intervention is social topology, not content.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy the Zoom comprehension dashboard with breakout grouping.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the virtual classroom tool; transfer 'assess-then-match humans' to other communities (gym buddy matching by plateau data, mentorship pairing in open source, grief support circles).
bestRemixTargets: Open-source contributor matchmaker pairing stuck newcomers with veterans based on issue activity
Language exchange circles auto-formed from measured conversation gaps
Company onboarding buddies matched from ticket/question patterns of new hires

sourceProductCards.name: Halo
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: bystander guidance during medical emergencies via AR and voice AI
sourceProductCards.concept: An iOS app that guides untrained bystanders through medical emergencies like CPR using spatial AR overlays and voice AI on a standard iPhone or iPad - no headset - so ordinary people can respond like trained professionals in the critical minutes before help arrives.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: In an emergency, the user points their iPhone at the scene; Halo overlays step-by-step AR visual guides and speaks instructions for actions like CPR in real time.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Bystanders freeze in medical emergencies because instructions from a 911 call are verbal-only and stressful to follow; formal training coverage is low and skills decay.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Ordinary untrained people present at a medical emergency
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Live camera view of the emergency scene plus the responder's voice interactions
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Spatial AR overlays showing where and how to act, synchronized with step-by-step spoken guidance
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Live AR-annotated camera view with voice-guided emergency procedure steps
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Swift-native iOS app blending Voice AI with spatial AR overlays: the camera view is annotated with placement and action guides (e.g. hand position and compression rhythm for CPR) while a voice agent delivers and paces the verbal protocol - designed to run on standard iPhones/iPads with no specialized headset.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Open app during a crisis, point the phone, follow the overlaid visuals and spoken steps until responders arrive.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It converts protocol knowledge into spatially-anchored, real-time coaching at the exact moment of need - the 'expertise on tap in a crisis' pattern with deliberately commodity hardware.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: First place in the Infosys Human Augmentation Track at HackHarvard 2025, with press coverage highlighting the founders' personal story (their mother's diabetic emergency).
adoptionOrAttentionProof: 1st Place, Infosys Human Augmentation Track, HackHarvard 2025
Press coverage by DT Next and Education Today
attentionProof: 1st Place, Infosys Human Augmentation Track, HackHarvard 2025
Press coverage by DT Next and Education Today
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: The 'crisis-moment AR + voice co-pilot for a rigid protocol' structure generalizes to many high-stakes, low-frequency tasks.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Two undergraduates' October 2025 hackathon project; no clinical validation or app-store product observed.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Anchor a step-by-step expert protocol to the physical scene through AR while a voice agent paces the user - expertise delivered at the moment and place of need.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Turn any bystander into the trained person in the room.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Spatial anchoring of emergency protocols on commodity phones instead of training people in advance.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy the AR CPR-guidance app.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the medical emergency app; transfer 'AR+voice protocol coaching at the moment of need' to other rare high-stakes tasks (childbirth support, fire extinguisher use, allergic reaction response, boat engine failure).
bestRemixTargets: Roadside breakdown copilot overlaying jack points and repair steps on your actual car
First-day-home newborn care coach with AR bathing/swaddling guidance
Home dialysis or injection assistant that visually verifies each setup step

sourceProductCards.name: ApprenticeCircle+
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: intergenerational skill-sharing and social connection for seniors aging at home
sourceProductCards.concept: A voice-first platform that connects adults 65+ with young adults through shared learning: seniors teach endangered crafts and cultural skills to youth anxious about the workforce, with onboarding done via a simple 10-minute phone conversation instead of any app setup.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: A senior has one 10-minute phone chat to create a profile; the platform then matches them with young learners for voice-mediated craft and skill apprenticeships.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Seniors aging at home lose social contact and their craft knowledge dies with them; simultaneously young adults lack mentors - and every digital solution fails seniors at the signup screen.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Adults 65+ living independently, and young adults seeking skills and mentorship
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: A natural 10-minute phone conversation from which the senior's profile, skills, and interests are extracted
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Matched intergenerational learning circles connecting seniors' skills with young learners
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Voice-accessible profile and match connections requiring no app literacy from the senior
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Voice-first onboarding: a conversational phone call is processed into a structured user profile (skills, crafts, interests, availability), eliminating forms entirely; matching then pairs seniors' at-risk cultural skills with young adults' learning goals, framed around Aalto's 'Aging with AI' ethical design constraints.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Senior talks on the phone once, then receives matched apprentices; young users learn crafts through recurring voice/meeting circles.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It inverts the elder-care framing - seniors are positioned as teachers with scarce knowledge, not care recipients - and treats a phone conversation as the entire onboarding UI.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Winner of Aalto University's 'Aging with AI' challenge at Junction 2025, Europe's leading hackathon (1,500 hackers, 291 projects), announced by Aalto's official news.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: Winner, Aalto University 'Aging with AI' challenge, Junction 2025 (1,500 participants, 291 projects)
Featured in Aalto University official news coverage
attentionProof: Winner, Aalto University 'Aging with AI' challenge, Junction 2025 (1,500 participants, 291 projects)
Featured in Aalto University official news coverage
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Two reusable moves: conversation-as-onboarding for tech-averse users, and reframing a 'needy' population as suppliers of scarce value.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Concept-stage hackathon win from November 2025 by a student team; no deployment observed.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Replace form-based onboarding with one recorded conversation parsed into a profile, then match users by complementary scarcity (one side's surplus knowledge to the other's demand).
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Talk for ten minutes, become a teacher.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Voice-only onboarding as the accessibility strategy plus intergenerational value exchange instead of one-way care.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy the senior-youth skill matching platform.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the senior apprenticeship platform; transfer 'conversation-parsed onboarding + scarcity matching' to other pairs (immigrant cooks and food-curious locals, retired tradespeople and DIY homeowners).
bestRemixTargets: Endangered-dialect conversation circles pairing elder native speakers with heritage learners
Retired-tradesperson hotline where a call creates a mentor profile for apprentice matching
Family recipe preservation service that interviews grandparents by phone and produces heirloom cookbooks

sourceProductCards.name: SoundClash
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: AI-hosted live music party quiz for venues and classrooms
sourceProductCards.concept: A zero-install multiplayer music party game: one screen hosts on a TV, players join from phones via QR code, and an AI host in 29 languages runs lyric-based mini-games (finish the line, misheard lyrics, beat timing, record-a-line-AI-sings-it) on live Musixmatch lyric data.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: Host picks artists/genres on a TV screen; players' phones become buzzers for AI-narrated lyric mini-games, with scores synced live across the room.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Music quiz nights need a human host, licensed content, and prepared questions; existing party games require app installs and can't adapt to any artist a crowd requests.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Bars, venues, classrooms, and house parties wanting an instant hosted music game
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Host's round setup (artist, genre, or song search, 3/6/9 rounds) plus players' phone taps and voice recordings during mini-games
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: AI-narrated quiz rounds with live lyric-based challenges, scoring, and crowd-rated AI-sung performances
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Synchronized TV host screen + per-player phone screens through a full game session
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Next.js rooms sync host TV and player phones via 1-second HTTP polling with no install; live Musixmatch APIs supply lyrics/metadata per chosen artist; Anthropic Claude generates plausible lyric distractors and localizes host banter into 29 languages; ElevenLabs provides host voice, music generation, and voice cloning for the 'Studio Session' game where a player records a line and AI sings it back; LALAL.AI does stem separation.
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Host creates room, players scan QR, rotating mini-games run in 3/6/9-round sets while an AI narrator drives pacing - multiple rooms in parallel, no player cap.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It composes four specialized music AI services into a coherent game loop where generation quality doesn't need to be perfect - wrong-but-plausible distractors and goofy AI singing are the fun, a rare 'AI imperfection as entertainment' design.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: One of three winners of Musixmatch's first Musicathon (523 developers, 100+ projects, June 2026), as reported by Music Ally.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: Winner (one of three), Musixmatch Musicathon 2026 (523 developers, 100+ projects)
attentionProof: Winner (one of three), Musixmatch Musicathon 2026 (523 developers, 100+ projects)
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Shows 'licensed live data + LLM distractor generation + voice AI' composed into a venue-shaped social game - and QR-join zero-install multiplayer as a distribution strategy.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Solo hackathon entry from June 2026 deployed on Railway; no game studio or venue distribution.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Generate wrong-but-plausible alternatives from a trusted content corpus to power quiz gameplay, hosted by a localized AI narrator, joined instantly via QR from phones.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: An AI quizmaster that can host any content, any language, any room, tonight.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: LLM-generated distractors against licensed ground-truth lyrics - the corpus guarantees the right answer, the model manufactures the game.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy the lyrics quiz party game.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the music quiz; transfer 'ground-truth corpus + generated distractors + AI host' to other corpora (art collections, sports stats, local history archives, film quotes).
bestRemixTargets: Museum-floor quiz night generated from the collection database with an AI docent host
Sports-bar trivia engine that builds rounds from tonight's actual game statistics
Classroom history quiz game generated from the week's curriculum documents

sourceProductCards.name: Lyri
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: AI-assisted songwriting and creative block relief
sourceProductCards.concept: An AI-powered songwriting assistant pitched at tackling writer's block, combining Musixmatch's lyrics analysis search API with Cyanite (music analysis), ElevenLabs (voice), and Lalal.ai (stem separation) to help songwriters develop ideas.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: A songwriter brings a stalled idea; the assistant draws on lyric-corpus analysis and audio AI services to propose directions that unblock the writing process.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Songwriters hit creative blocks with no structured way to explore how comparable songs solved the same lyrical or structural problem.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Songwriters and lyricists facing creative blocks
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: The songwriter's work-in-progress material and creative intent
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: AI-assisted songwriting suggestions grounded in lyric-corpus analysis and audio processing
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: Songwriting assistance session outputs (suggested lyrical directions/material)
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: Composes four specialized music AI APIs - Musixmatch lyrics analysis search for corpus grounding, Cyanite for music understanding, ElevenLabs for voice, and Lalal.ai for stem separation - into a songwriting assistant workflow targeting writer's block (per Music Ally's winner coverage; detailed internal flow not publicly documented).
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Iterative co-writing: bring stuck material, receive corpus-informed suggestions, refine.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It grounds generative songwriting help in analysis of a licensed professional lyrics corpus rather than free-form LLM generation - suggestion by reference, not hallucination.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Named one of three winners of Musixmatch's first Musicathon (523 developers, 100+ projects) in Music Ally's coverage.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: Winner (one of three), Musixmatch Musicathon 2026 (523 developers, 100+ projects)
attentionProof: Winner (one of three), Musixmatch Musicathon 2026 (523 developers, 100+ projects)
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Candidate signal for 'corpus-grounded creative assistance' in music; mechanism detail is thin, so held as candidate rather than core source.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Hackathon winner from June 2026; no product page or distribution observed.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Ground a creative assistant's suggestions in analytical search over a licensed professional corpus so proposals cite how real works solved the same problem.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Unblock creators by showing how the canon solved it.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Writer's-block positioning backed by lyric-corpus analytics instead of open-ended generation.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy a generic AI lyric generator.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the songwriting assistant; transfer 'corpus-analytics-grounded creative unblocking' to other crafts (screenwriting beats, stand-up comedy structure, poetry forms).
bestRemixTargets: Screenwriter's beat-block assistant grounded in a produced-screenplay corpus
Poetry form coach that shows how canonical poems executed the structure you're attempting

sourceProductCards.name: VeriStream: Fact-First in Every Frame
sourceProductCards.originalDomain: real-time misinformation detection and correction in live broadcasts
sourceProductCards.concept: A system by Team Alchemist (Dehradun) that detects and corrects misinformation inside live broadcast streams, combining LangChain-powered NLP, dynamic knowledge graphs, GIS insights, and explainable AI so every flagged claim comes with an inspectable rationale.
sourceProductCards.oneLineDescription: A live broadcast feed goes in; flagged claims with knowledge-graph-backed verdicts, geographic context, and explainable rationales come out in near real time.
sourceProductCards.problemSolved: Misinformation during live events spreads before traditional fact-checking can respond; broadcasters lack in-stream tooling that both flags and explains falsehoods as they air.
sourceProductCards.targetUser: Broadcasters and newsroom verification teams covering live events
sourceProductCards.coreUserInput: Live broadcast audio/video streams containing spoken and on-screen claims
sourceProductCards.coreOutput: Real-time flags on suspect claims with corrections, supporting evidence, and explainable reasoning
sourceProductCards.outputArtifact: In-stream verification layer with per-claim explanations for editorial review
sourceProductCards.coreMechanism: LangChain-orchestrated NLP extracts claims from the live stream; claims are checked against dynamic knowledge graphs updated as events unfold; GIS analysis adds geographic plausibility signals; an explainable-AI layer exposes why each claim was flagged, enabling on-air correction rather than silent moderation (as described in the Government of India PIB winners announcement).
sourceProductCards.interactionPattern: Verification desk monitors the flag feed during live coverage, reviews explanations, and pushes corrections into the broadcast.
sourceProductCards.whyItIsInteresting: It targets the live window where fact-checks currently cannot exist, and makes explainability the editorial interface - journalists need the 'why' to act on-air within seconds.
sourceProductCards.whyItGotAttention: Selected among the top 5 winners of the TruthTell Hackathon announced by India's Ministry of Information & Broadcasting ahead of the WAVES Summit 2025.
adoptionOrAttentionProof: Top-5 Winner, TruthTell Hackathon (Create in India Challenge, WAVES Summit 2025), announced by Government of India PIB
Named in the official PIB press release
attentionProof: Top-5 Winner, TruthTell Hackathon (Create in India Challenge, WAVES Summit 2025), announced by Government of India PIB
Named in the official PIB press release
sourceProductCards.reasonIncluded: Rare journalism-domain winner with a concrete verification chain (claims to knowledge graph to explainable verdict) operating on live streams rather than static articles.
sourceProductCards.reasonNotMajorProduct: Hackathon-stage system from a Dehradun student/indie team, April 2025; no newsroom deployment observed.
sourceProductCards.transferableStructure: Extract claims from a live stream, verify against a continuously updated knowledge graph, and surface explainable verdicts fast enough for in-event intervention.
sourceProductCards.ideaKernel: Fact-checking at broadcast latency, with reasons attached.
sourceProductCards.noveltyKernel: Dynamic knowledge graphs plus GIS plausibility for live events - verification that updates as fast as the story.
sourceProductCards.cloneRisk: A lazy clone would copy a generic real-time fake-news detector.
sourceProductCards.antiCloneBoundary: Do not copy the broadcast fact-checker; transfer 'live claim extraction + updating knowledge graph + explainable verdicts' to other live speech settings (earnings calls, sports commentary accuracy, town-hall meetings, livestream shopping claims).
bestRemixTargets: Livestream-shopping claim checker that flags exaggerated product claims in real time
Earnings-call verifier cross-checking executive statements against filings as they speak
Local council meeting monitor that fact-checks budget claims against public records live

coverageGaps: Fashion and crafts/maker domains: no verifiable 2025-2026 hackathon winner with observable mechanics found this pass (closest adjacent: ApprenticeCircle+ covers craft preservation).
Personal finance: FinHacks 2025 projects were beginner-level with no observable award pages; no card taken.

Culture/museums: AI4Culture (Feb 2025) and culture.explore(data) (Oct 2025) hackathons exist but winner project pages with mechanics were not locatable; HackaLOD winner found was from 2022 (too old, excluded).
Junction 2025 main-prize winner WAVELET: award confirmed by multiple sources but no public description of the project's mechanics found; dropped rather than fabricate.
Hack the North 2025: winner list not published in structured form on Devpost gallery search results; individual finalist mechanics unverifiable this pass.
Music creation tools beyond the Musixmatch Musicathon (e.g. Sonar AI & Music festival hackathon winners) not verifiable for 2025.
editorNotes: NASA Space Apps 2025 intentionally skipped per brief (already covered in index).

Google Chrome Built-in AI Challenge 2025 (announced Nov 2025, 1,300+ submissions) contributes 7 cards; justified because each occupies a distinct subject domain (motor accessibility, AAC, pronunciation, pets, outdoors, neurodivergent education, games) and all were verified at evidence level A via fetched Devpost project pages.
AI-agent/devtools cap respected: only FaceTimeOS (computer-use agent) is agent-domain; HawkWatch/VeriStream are security/journalism applications.

PIB press release for TruthTell winners (VeriStream) returned HTTP 403 on direct fetch; its contents were observed via search-result rendering of the same PRID page. Card kept at evidenceLevel B with medium confidence.
Lyri kept as candidate_only: award is confirmed by Music Ally but internal mechanism detail is thin; do not treat its coreMechanism as observed.
Musixmatch Musicathon is a June 2026 event - the most recent corpus entries here; SoundClash mechanics observed directly from its public GitHub README and live demo link.
Devpost hackathon subdomain submission URLs 302-redirect to devpost.com/software/* canonical URLs; canonical URLs are used as card urls.
FridgeFit and Shot Analyzer are PhysTech 2025 honorable-mention winners (the event lists a winner pool rather than a single grand prize); placement wording kept exact to avoid overclaiming.

editorShortlist.title: ドジャース 大谷翔平 22号ホームラン 日米通算350号に到達
editorShortlist.summary: 大リーグ、ドジャースの大谷翔平選手が12日、オールスターゲーム前最後の試合となるダイヤモンドバックス戦に1番・指名打者で先発出場し、1回の第1打席で初球を振り抜いて2試合ぶりとなる22号のソロホームランを打ちました。プロ野球の日本ハム時代に打った48本と合わせて日米通算350号に到達しました。

editorShortlist.title: 西日本では連日の「危険な暑さ」予想 熱中症対策の徹底を
editorShortlist.summary: 13日も西日本を中心に気温が上がり、九州では最高気温が38度と危険な暑さが予想されています。エアコンを適切に使い、こまめに水分補給するなど、熱中症対策を徹底してください。
